description: Python ecosystem groupings (pep621/uv/poetry). Peer-dep coupled stacks bundled together.

packageRules.description: Don't track `python` via the native pep621 manager — `requires-python` is normally a min-version metadata range (`>=3.10`), not a runtime pin, so the pep621 view of it must not bump. Exact `==X.Y.Z` pins ARE tracked, but via the custom manager below + the `python-version` datasource, which surfaces a separate `python` dep that this rule does not touch.

Group the exact-pinned `requires-python` (custom manager below, depName `python`) into the same PR as the `python` Docker base image (dockerfile manager, also depName `python`) so an exact CPython pin and its base image never bump separately and drift apart.

customManagers.description: Track an EXACT-pinned `requires-python = "==X.Y.Z"` in pyproject.toml against the CPython runtime (datasource `python-version`, depName `python`) so it bumps in lockstep with the `python` Docker base image. Anchored to the `==`-triple form only: floor ranges (`>=3.10`), compatible ranges (`~=3.14`), and wildcard pins (`==3.14.*`) do NOT match, so non-exact-pin consumers are a no-op. The `==` is literal context outside the capture; only the `X.Y.Z` triple is replaced. uv.lock's own `requires-python` may lag this edit until lockfile maintenance reconciles it — pair with a `UV_PYTHON_DOWNLOADS=never` build guard.
